name: Emergency Response Simulator
description: Scenario-based training for laser emergency situations

emergency_scenarios.title: Aircraft Incursion During Show
emergency_scenarios.description: An aircraft enters the protected airspace while lasers are active.

incorrect_actions.consequence: Aircraft enters beam path. Laser illumination incident occurs.
Manual redirection too slow. Incident still occurs.

correct_action.description: Confirm all lasers off, track aircraft
All observers confirm clear airspace before restart

debrief_points: Emergency stop activation time is critical
Never wait for confirmation before shutdown
Verification comes after shutdown, not before
Communication with ATC maintains safety awareness
Restart requires all-clear from all observers

emergency_scenarios.title: Scan-Failure Protection Malfunction
emergency_scenarios.description: The scan-failure protection system fails during operation.

decision_points.prompt: The scan-failure alarm has sounded, but the lasers continue projecting. What is your immediate action?

options.text: Activate emergency stop immediately
options.feedback: Correct. Any scan-failure malfunction requires immediate shutdown.

options.text: Test if backup protection is working
options.feedback: Never test systems while beams are active and audience is present.

options.text: Continue monitoring - beams appear normal
options.feedback: Scan-failure malfunction is a critical safety issue requiring immediate action.
options.consequence: Potential injury to audience or aircraft

options.text: Reduce beam power and continue
options.feedback: Reducing power doesn't address the fundamental safety system failure.

options.text: Inspect equipment before considering restart
options.feedback: Correct. Equipment must be inspected and repaired before restart.

options.text: Reset system and restart show
options.feedback: Never restart without identifying and correcting the malfunction cause.

options.text: Disable protection and continue manually
options.feedback: Operating without scan-failure protection is never acceptable.

debrief_points: Scan-failure protection is a critical safety system
Malfunction requires immediate shutdown
Equipment must be inspected before restart
Never operate without functioning protection systems
Document all malfunctions for maintenance records

emergency_scenarios.title: Pilot Reports Laser Illumination
emergency_scenarios.description: A pilot reports being illuminated by a laser. Authorities are investigating.

initial_state.your_beams: Were directed away from that location

decision_points.prompt: You receive a call from the FAA about a laser illumination report. How do you respond?

options.text: Cooperate fully, provide all documentation
options.feedback: Correct. Full cooperation is legally required and professionally essential.

options.text: Deny your lasers could be responsible
options.feedback: Never make statements without reviewing evidence and documentation.

options.text: Refuse to speak until consulting lawyer
options.feedback: While legal counsel is your right, complete cooperation is the professional standard.

decision_points.prompt: What documentation should you immediately gather?

options.text: FAA approval letter (LOD)
Active NOTAM documentation
Observer logs for the time period
Weather conditions at time of report
Equipment settings and beam directions
Evidence of other laser activity in area
options.feedback: Focus on your documentation, not deflection.

decision_points.prompt: Investigators ask about your beam directions at the time. How do you respond?

options.text: Show them the recorded beam path data and observer logs
options.feedback: Correct. Accurate records are your best defense.

options.text: Describe from memory where beams were pointing
options.feedback: Never rely on memory. Always refer to documented records.

options.text: Say you're not sure - beams move around a lot
options.feedback: This suggests lack of control. Proper operations require tracking.

debrief_points: Documentation is your best defense
Cooperate fully with investigations
Never speculate or make statements from memory
Accurate records prove compliance
Investigations can take months - maintain all records

emergency_scenarios.title: Medical Emergency On Site
emergency_scenarios.description: A crew member suffers a medical emergency requiring evacuation.

decision_points.prompt: An observer collapses with chest pain. What's your first action?

options.text: Call 911/EMS immediately
options.feedback: Correct. Medical emergency takes immediate priority.

options.text: Continue show while someone else handles it
options.feedback: Medical emergency requires your immediate attention as LSO.

options.text: Emergency stop lasers, then call 911
options.feedback: Also acceptable - securing the show is appropriate when crew member down.

decision_points.prompt: EMS needs clear access. How do you handle this?

options.text: Order show shutdown, clear evacuation route
options.feedback: Correct. Show must yield to emergency medical needs.

options.text: Continue show, route around active area
options.feedback: Never prioritize show over emergency medical access.

options.text: Pause briefly, resume when EMS arrives
options.feedback: Medical emergency requires full shutdown until resolved.

decision_points.prompt: With one observer down, how do you manage coverage?

options.text: Redeploy remaining observers, accept reduced coverage
options.feedback: Reduced coverage may not be safe. Consider shutdown.

options.text: Emergency shutdown - insufficient crew for safe operation
options.feedback: Correct. Safe operations require adequate crew.

options.text: Call backup observer and continue
options.feedback: Backup won't arrive in time. Shutdown is appropriate.

debrief_points: Medical emergencies take priority over the show
EMS needs immediate, unimpeded access
Staffing levels must be maintained for safety
Shutdown may be necessary even if show is going well
Have emergency action plan for medical situations

excellent.message: Excellent response! Your actions demonstrate professional-level emergency management.

good.message: Good response. Some areas for improvement identified.

needs_improvement.message: Review needed. Several responses could compromise safety.

critical_review.message: Critical safety issues identified. Mandatory review required.
